_publications._publicationTitle: Biochemical and structural analyses reveal that the tumor suppressor neurofibromin (NF1) forms a high-affinity dimer.
_publications._context: Entry clones were produced by PCR amplification of portions of the NF1 gene from Addgene construct 70423, which contains a codon-optimized version of human NF1 isoform 2 | Point mutations in NF1 isoform-2 were generated by PCR mutagenesis of Addgene plasmid 70423.

_publications._publicationTitle: Destabilizing NF1 variants act in a dominant negative manner through neurofibromin dimerization.
